The '''''Arc Hammer''''' was an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] spacefaring production facility used to supplement the Imperial [[Stormtrooper Corps/Legends|Stormtrooper Corps]] with [[Dark Trooper/Legends|dark troopers]], a new generation of [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] to be used in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] against the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]].<ref name="SW:DF" />

==Characteristics==
[[File:ExecutorArcHammer4.png|thumb|left|200px|In formation with the [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']], an [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] passes in front of the ''Arc Hammer''.]]
The ''Arc Hammer'' was a [[factory ship]] of a unique design. Like many other [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Starship/Legends|starships]], the ''Arc Hammer'' had a long, narrow, dagger-shaped hull; however, it also had four large columns surrounding a central one that protruded from below and a small, flat bridge structure in front of the columns on its dorsal side.<ref name="SW:DF" />

The ''Arc Hammer'' served as a primary testbed of the [[Dark Trooper Project]], a brainchild of [[General/Legends|General]] [[Rom Mohc]] which was to create larger, more powerful [[Droid/Legends|robotic versions]] of stormtroopers. The Dark Troopers were developed inside the vessel. Construction lines filled the interior and four large ventral hangars were responsible for receiving cargo vessels and loading batches of dark troopers into [[Transport/Legends|transports]] or deploying them directly above a target world.<ref name="SW:DF" />

Though not its purpose, the ''Arc Hammer'' was equipped with enough weaponry to defend itself against attack from space. The inside of the ''Arc Hammer'' contained crewed sections that were visually similar to that of other Imperial ship designs. However, unlike the Empire's dedicated warships, a significant portion of the ''Arc Hammer''<nowiki/>'s interior was occupied by maze-like industrial production facilities, including automated component fabrication, assembly lines, conveyer belts, and other such machinery, as its primary purpose was construction of Dark Trooper droids and powered exoskeletal armor suits.<ref name="SW:DF" />
Defense of its interior against boarders or saboteurs was composed of more common Imperial troops like stormtroopers, but this was augmented by the presence of active Dark Trooper units.

The ''Arc Hammer'' is known to have cost billions of credits to construct.<ref name="EGTD" />

==History==
[[File:ArcHammerDestruction-DFRemaster.png|thumb|left|200px|The ''Arc Hammer'' is destroyed.]]
The ''Arc Hammer'' was a titanic space faring construction facility constructed at [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]] and was classified as a [[Dreadnaught/Legends|dreadnaught]].<ref name="TEGTW" /> After the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|destruction]] of the newest Imperial superweapon, the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], the Empire started to share General Rom Mohc's views, who considered that all soldiers should be given a chance to face each other in a one-on-one combat. Mohc eventually persuaded the Imperial officials to greenlight the [[Dark Trooper Project]], which involved creating [[Droid/Legends|droid]] versions of Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]. To this end, he was provided with the ''Arc Hammer''.<ref name="EGTD" />

In order to ensure secrecy of the project, the factory frequently made jumps to [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], and only one ship, the [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']], knew its exact position at any given time. The Dark Trooper project proceeded quickly, and by [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] the first working prototypes were made. To demonstrate the power of the Dark Troopers to the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]], it was arranged to attack the Rebel [[Tak Base]] on the planet [[Talay/Legends|Talay]]. The demonstration was successful; only one [[Grey Damsel|Rebel ship]] managed to escape from the planet, although it was boarded by a Dark Trooper as well, who then killed all crew aboard. The base itself was destroyed with all hands. However, before its destruction, [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Toka]] managed to send a distress signal.<ref name="SW:DF" /><ref name="Sand Blasted" />

[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]]-allied [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] [[Kyle Katarn]] was assigned to investigate the loss of Tak Base. While exploring the destroyed base, Kyle discovered a large blaster weapon, much too bulky to be wielded by a regular stormtrooper.<ref name="SW:DF" /> This investigation trail led to several different Imperial weapons research centers and industrial facilities spread throughout the galaxy, eventually leading the Rebel agent to discover the Dark Trooper Project. Ultimately, Katarn [[Mission to the Arc Hammer|infiltrated the ''Arc Hammer'']] by stowing away on a materials cargo transfer, and sabotaged the ship by placing [[sequencer charge]]s on several power couplings within the ship, leading to its destruction.<ref name="SW:DF" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Arc_Hammer_Sandblasted.jpg|200px|thumb|A ventral view from "[[Sand Blasted]]," with some notable differences from the [[Star Wars: Dark Forces|''Dark Forces'']] appearance.]]

The length of the ''Arc Hammer'' is not explicitly given, but can be determined from visuals by comparing it to ''Executor'' and several escorting vessels of known length in the ''Dark Forces'' cinematics. It is shown clearly to be about half the length (and slightly smaller) than the ''Executor'', albeit being further away from the camera than the [[Star Dreadnought/Legends|Star Dreadnought]].

Another way to calculate it more precisely is to compare it with the [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyers]] that are closer to the camera than it. The ''Arc Hammer'' is at least 6 times the size of a Imperial-class Star Destroyer that is closer to the camera; therefore, the ''Arc Hammer'' has a length that is definitely greater than 9.6 [[Kilometer/Legends|km]].

[[File:ArcHammer_EofW.jpg|thumb|left|200px|''Arc Hammer'' from ''Empire at War'']]

''[[Star Wars: Empire at War: Forces of Corruption]]'' has the ''Arc Hammer'' contradictorily portrayed at a much smaller fraction of the ''Executor''{{'s}} size, roughly the size of an [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]]. The reasoning behind this is that the ship's role is as a factory and research facility and would therefore not need to be as big as the ''Executor''.<ref name="LucasForums">{{WebCite|url=http://www.lucasforums.com/showpost.php?p=2186023&postcount=67|text=LucasForums' post by Darth_Torpid-PG, Petroglyph developer|archivedate=20071014070156}}</ref> The game's Prima guide claims the ship is the size of a Star Destroyer, but since the term "Star Destroyer" (without a specific class designation) has been used to describe ships in size from the ''[[Demolisher]]'' to the [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']], this provides no clear estimate.

"[[Sand Blasted]]" shows a different and inconsistent version of the factory, which resembles the traditional wedge-shaped Star Destroyer profile, albeit with two column-shaped structures protruding from the hull.
